asi.lessons: Small handler modules with repetitive `#[tracing::instrument]` annotations are still good ROI on the vendored openraft frontier: explicit entered spans plus saturating purge math removed nearly the whole file-local tail without public API churn. Late broad-workspace gains are smaller than the first big vendored slices, but they are still measurable and likely real on the corrected parser.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the broad rail going with another medium-sized mechanical vendored file rather than jumping straight into `raft_core.rs`. Best next candidates remain `openraft/openraft/src/config/config.rs`, `openraft/openraft/src/core/sm/worker.rs`, or `openraft/openraft/src/raft/raft_inner.rs`; prefer whichever shows the highest ratio of mechanical warnings to API churn after a quick local inspection.

description: Cleaned `openraft/openraft/src/core/sm/worker.rs` for the corrected broad workspace rail: replaced `#[tracing::instrument]` on the async worker helpers with explicit spans, made ignored response sends log dropped receivers instead of discarding results, and switched the remaining `last.index() + 1` / `end - 1` math to saturating helpers. Targeted `cargo check -p openraft`, `cargo test -p openraft install_full_snapshot_test --lib`, and `cargo test -p openraft trigger_snapshot_test --lib` stayed green, and the corrected fresh-target-dir full-workspace summary-index metric dropped from 452 to 443 (-9).

asi.lessons: For the corrected broad workspace parser, summary-index lines are indented (`  F149 ...`), so the parser must match leading whitespace before `F<number>`. The worker cleanup was a real win: targeted openraft check/tests stayed green and the file’s remaining broad-rail tail fell to a single `unbounded_loop` hit on the main receive loop.
asi.next_action_hint: Stay on the corrected broad rail and keep targeting medium-size vendored openraft files with many mechanical findings before tackling `raft_core.rs`. asi.lessons: For this summary-index parser, the stable regex must allow leading spaces before `F<number>` and capture the four-column format (`F lint crate path:line`). `engine_impl.rs` was excellent ROI: most of its visible tail was attr-macro noise plus a handful of mechanical unwrap/result/default issues, so one focused pass cut the full-workspace metric materially while targeted openraft validation stayed green.

asi.lessons: For this broad rail, medium-size vendored modules continue to yield honest double-digit wins when the work stays on typed input structs, explicit loop bounds, and checked/saturating arithmetic. Also, package-local validation for the replication slice needs the replication-handler unit rails (`append_membership_test`, `update_matching_test`) rather than unrelated snapshot tests.
